_sources.ovx: CBOE CDN OVX_History.csv - D0 uses Feb 27 close (Feb 28 = Saturday); D7/D8 carry D6 (weekend, market closed); D10 live via /api/cboe
_sources.vxeem: CBOE CDN VXEEM_History.csv - all D0-D9 confirmed from live data; D7/D8 carry D6 (weekend); D10 live via /api/cboe
_sources.scenarios: D10-D12 confirmed from banker-conflict.json; all prior days estimated from narrative context

days.note: Sun - market closed; scenarios carried from D74

Mon - Trump calls off planned Iran strike on Truth Social at Gulf states' request (Saudi, UAE, Qatar); 'serious negotiations' toward peace deal (CBS News, WashPost); Brent fell >1% to ~$110.69 (CNBC); WTI $108.21 (-0.41%); oil stayed elevated despite postponement - supply uncertainty unchanged; IEA warns 'rapidly shrinking buffers' may herald future price spikes; tail eases to 37%

Tue - Markets digest Trump postponement; CNBC: 'investors weigh mixed signals from Trump on whether US will resume Iran war'; Brent morning $112.93 (Fortune 9:20am ET intraday); settled lower as postponement digested; IEA warns shrinking buffers; tail 37% carried

Wed - Trump declares Iran talks 'in final stages'; WTI falls below $100 (CNBC); Brent morning $110.34 (Fortune 9:30am ET); IRGC claims 26 vessels coordinated through Hormuz in 24hrs (Al Jazeera); Iran publishes new PGSA controlled maritime zone (Jask to Siri Island; IRGC authorization required); deal optimism drives de-escalation pricing; tail 34%

Thu - Iran reviews US peace proposal; Trump says willing to wait 'a few days' for 'right answers' (CNBC); Rubio: 'some encouraging signs' of potential agreement; Brent volatile: morning $104.20 (-0.8%), rebounded to $106.92 (+1.9%), fell again; JKM $18.92 confirmed (+0.08%, Canada LNG Group); WTI morning $97.66, rebounded $100.59; tail 34% maintained

Fri - Khamenei directive orders Iran uranium to remain on Iranian soil (May 22, reports); deal hopes partially reversed; Brent $104.52 (+1.89%, TradingEconomics confirmed); WTI $97.12 (-0.90%, Investing.com confirmed); TTF €47.69 (-3.48%, TradingEconomics confirmed; Norwegian exports +40% Troll maintenance ends); Gold $4,524.05 (-0.42%, TradingEconomics confirmed); JKM $18.92 carried (Canada LNG Group May 21); UK deploys HMS Dragon/Typhoons/Kraken to multinational Hormuz mission (40+ nations); CENTCOM: 94 vessels turned away cumulatively; tail 38%

Sat - Trump declares Iran MOU 'largely negotiated' (CNBC, CNN, NPR); 60-day Phase 1 framework announced: Hormuz reopens, no tolls, Iran clears mines, Iran oil sales resume, US lifts blockade + sanctions waivers; Phase 2 = nuclear talks 30-60 days; markets closed but oil futures moved sharply lower in Asian hours; tail collapses from 38% to 25% on deal signal

Sun - Deal details emerge (Axios May 24, CNN May 24); Iran's Fars: Strait 'under Iran management' disputes US framing; nuclear sequencing still unresolved; Brent -7% to ~$96 on Hormuz reopening hope; tail 23%

Mon - Mixed signals (Al Jazeera: 'Oil prices fall amid mixed signals on US-Iran peace deal'); Brent ~$97 est.; WTI fell 'below $91' on deal optimism (TradingEconomics); TTF €46.63 (-4.22%, TradingEconomics) rebound from Troll surge; scenarios held at D83

Tue - Brent $98.21 (+0.99%, TradingEconomics confirmed); WTI $92.58 (+1.74%, TradingEconomics confirmed); Gold $4,530.61 (-0.85%, TradingEconomics confirmed); TTF €46.71 (+2.47%, TradingEconomics confirmed); JKM $18.81 est. (Platts May 22 carried; Canada LNG Group May 25 weekly not yet published); iTraxx Asia IG ~178bp (-6bp est.); ASEAN HY ~550bp (-13bp est.); deal 'largely negotiated' but nuclear sequencing + Strait sovereignty disputed; tail 23%
